title: Leticia Hernandez case — Oceanside PD cold case
note: The geographic seed for the grove: abducted in Oceanside (Dec 1989), remains found March 1991 in a brush-filled canyon off Pala–Temecula Road (Highway S16) near the Pala reservation. Official Oceanside PD unsolved-case page. Real, unsolved, and owed respect — the novel borrows the geography and the county's silence, not the person.

title: San Diego serial murders (1985–1990) — Wikipedia
note: The factual anchor for the inversion and the 'nobodies' theme: dozens of women — many sex workers, many marginalized — killed in and around San Diego; a belated task force; documented institutional failure; most cases never resolved as one killer's work. Read for how a county files certain victims, which is the novel's whole argument.

title: Escondido, California — Wikipedia
note: The model city for 'Escondida' (the name means 'hidden' — keep the meaning even if you rename). Useful for 1990s population, the avocado/citrus economy, geography of the inland valley, and distances to Pala/Pauma along SR-76.

title: Bureau of Justice Statistics (DOJ)
note: Period policing and crime data for 1994 verisimilitude — search the site for 'Criminal Victimization 1994' and 'Law Enforcement Management and Administrative Statistics' (LEMAS, 1993). Linked to the homepage because BJS deep links change; use the site search.

title: NYT archive search — San Diego County, 1994
note: This is a SEARCH LINK, not a specific article — a starting point for period reporting on North County crime, the early-90s recession, water politics, and avocado economics. The archive (paywalled) is the best single source for how the era talked about itself.

title: John Truby — The Anatomy of Story
note: The moral-argument school of structure — exactly right for a book whose theme ('truth and justice are not the same thing') must be argued through character choices, not stated. Truby's 'web of characters' chapter maps cleanly onto Vega/Brennan/Lucia/Crane as four positions on one theme.

title: John Yorke — Into the Woods: A Five-Act Journey Into Story
note: The best structure book written from inside television — Yorke ran UK drama, and his five-act logic is the natural bridge from the novel's 8 beats to an 8-episode limited series. His fractal principle (acts within scenes within episodes) is the tool for making each interrogation session a complete shape.

title: Jessica Brody — Save the Cat! Writes a Novel
note: The 15-beat sheet adapted for novelists. Use as a cross-check, not a template: map the 8-chapter spine against her beats to find soft spots (our 'Fun and Games' equivalent is the ch. 2–3 investigation; our 'Dark Night' is ch. 7). Her 'Whydunit' genre chapter is directly on point.

title: Unreliable narrator — Wikipedia
note: Taxonomy and canon examples. Vega is the rare deliberate unreliable narrator — not deluded but curating — which puts him closer to Ford Madox Ford's Dowell or Ishiguro's Stevens than to the twist-narrators. The fair-play discipline (reader gets the evidence, narrator supplies the spin) is the craft standard to hold.

title: Frame story — Wikipedia
note: The 2024 interrogation is a frame narrative whose frame is itself a plot (the hunt). Survey of how frames create meaning — and the key structural question for ch. 8: what happens when the frame and the framed story collide in real time.

title: Nonlinear narrative — Wikipedia
note: Dual-timeline mechanics: when to alternate at chapter level vs scene level (the novel tightens from chapter-level braiding to scene-level braiding as it accelerates into the nadir), and how revelation order — not event order — carries a mystery.

title: Detective fiction — Wikipedia
note: The Dupin/Holmes lineage Vega descends from — the ratiocinative detective — and the hardboiled counter-tradition the setting descends from. The novel's game is putting a Dupin mind inside a Chandler county and letting the county win.

title: Film adaptation — Wikipedia
note: Overview of novel-to-screen adaptation and its fidelity debates. For the 8-episode plan, the working rule: adapt the revelation order, not the prose — the S16 canvass catch, for instance, is a novel trick that must become an actable beat (Cardenas's notebook) on screen.

title: Dirty Plush — the manuscript
note: The working draft lives outside the vault. Set novel.onedrive_url (ms-word launcher) and/or novel.web_url in writers-panel-config.json to wire this entry to your manuscript; the app runs fine with both blank.
